title: Epigaea rubicunda
taxonomy: Epigaea rubicunda is a species within the genus Epigaea. It belongs to the family Ericaceae. The family Ericaceae is commonly referred to as the heath family. intro: Epigaea rubicunda, also known as Trailing Arbutus or Mayflower, is a low-growing, evergreen perennial plant native to Eastern North America, characterized by its pink or white, bell-shaped flowers and dark green, leathery leaves. The health benefits of Epigaea rubicunda include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Epigaea rubicunda is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used to support respiratory health, particularly in the treatment of coughs and bronchitis.
